zh.Paston, Norfolkshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓
英國英格蘭林可爾史爾凱斯托
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Beauchamp於1362年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓時，她的父親Roger de Beauchamp27歲，母親Joan Clopton22歲。她和Robert Mautbey至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1482年6月3日之前。

zh-hans.Paston, Norfolkshire, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可柏斯頓
英国英格兰林可爾史爾凱斯托
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Beauchamp于1362年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可柏斯頓时，她的父亲Roger de Beauchamp27岁，母亲Joan Clopton22岁。她和Robert Mautbey至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1482年6月3日之前。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Beauchamp was born in 1362, in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger de Beauchamp, was 27 and her mother, Joan Clopton, was 22. She had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Robert Mautbey. She died before 3 June 1482.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Beauchamp est née en 1362, à Paston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Beauchamp, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan Clopton, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Robert Mautbey. Elle est décédée avant 3 juin 1482.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Beauchamp 1362 in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Beauchamp 27 und ihre Mutter Joan Clopton 22. Sie hatte mit Robert Mautbey mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 3. Juni 1482.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1362 nel Paston, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger de Beauchamp, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Joan Clopton, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Robert Mautbey. Morì prima del 3 giugno 1482.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Beauchampが1362年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクプアストンで生まれた時，父Roger de Beauchampは27歳，母Joan Cloptonは22歳であった。彼女はRobert Mautbeyとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1482年6月3日以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Beauchamp(이)가 1362년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 파스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Beauchamp(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Clopton(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Robert Mautbey(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1482년 6월 3일 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Beauchamp nasceu em 1362, em Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger de Beauchamp, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Joan Clopton, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Robert Mautbey. Ela faleceu antes 3 de junho de 1482.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Beauchamp родилась в 1362 г. в местности пастон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger de Beauchamp было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Clopton, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Robert Mautbey. Она умерла перед 3 июнь 1482 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Beauchamp nació en 1362, en Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger de Beauchamp, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Joan Clopton, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con Robert Mautbey. Murió antes de 3 de junio de 1482.
